title: Reis soul in 30
wikitext:
[]: Rei's soul in 3.0?             Quote Post by qwdc » Sun Jun 23, 2013 2:16 pm I've only watched 3.0 in it's entirety once, so I'm not sure if this was addressed at all during the movie: does the Rei clone still have Lilith's soul? Actually, considering how much Rebuild has deviated from the original anime, is it even safe to assume that Rei at any point served as the vessel for Lilith's soul? Since Lilith's corpse took on Rei's form, it is not a stretch to say that Rei's original soul (which may or may not be Lilith's soul) at least made contact with Lilith during Third Impact. However, this leaves the current whereabouts of her original soul in question: is it still in EVA 01, trapped in Lilith's rotting body or was it extracted and put into the new Rei?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jun 23, 2013 2:23 pm Have you checked out the 3.33 FAQ , there's also a discussion thread about those questions in the FAQ stickied in this subforum.

[]:             Quote Post by qwdc » Sun Jun 23, 2013 2:38 pm Thanks! This FAQ did help me answer a few questions. I do remember Kaworu implying that Rei Q was soulless. This still makes me wonder though, if Rei II did make contact with Lilith consider how she should be fused with EVA 01. I guess it is possible that EVA 01 made contact with Lilith at some point before 3.0, but I guess that's a question hopefully to be answered during FINAL.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Jun 23, 2013 5:30 pm qwdc wrote: I do remember Kaworu implying that Rei Q was soulless.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jun 23, 2013 5:37 pm Well, according to Reichu, the Japanese wording was very ambiguous(probably on purpose), it could mean that her soul was in a different place inside her body than normal Lilin, or that her soul is literally somewhere else than in her body.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jun 24, 2013 4:35 am Even so, it suggests to me the continued existence of a soul somehow associated with ReiQ, even if its relationship with the body that we see is uncertain.
